intro: Stillingia widgrenii, also known as Devil's Back Bone, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stillingia widgrenii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
